title: vicia lens subsp. orientalis
intro: Vicia lens subsp. orientalis, also known as the oriental vetch, is a sub-species of the vetch plant that belongs to the legume family. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The herbal preparations of Vicia lens subsp. orientalis include decoctions, infusions, and topical creams, which are often used in traditional medicine practices.
